revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Inabane-ōji]][[ja:稲葉根王子]]
{{Infobox religious building |image = File:Inabaneoji - Feb 19, 2006.jpg |deity={{ill|Inari|qid=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}|prefecture={{ill|Kamitonda|qid=Q1346882|1=Kamitonda, Wakayama}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.71275|N|135.45388888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Gotai-ōji|qid=Q3111472|1=Gotai-ōji}}; {{ill|Kujūku-ōji|qid=Q3200280|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines}}}}
'''Inabane Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
== Inabane Ōji == '''Inabane Ōji''' (稲葉根王子) is a {{nihongo|Shinto shrine|神社|jinja}} located in {{ill|Kamitonda, Wakayama|en|Kamitonda, Wakayama|qid=Q1346882|lt=Kamitonda, Wakayama|1=Kamitonda, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}, Japan. Situated along the {{nihongo|Nakahechi route|中辺路}} of the {{nihongo|Kumano Kōdō|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}}, it is one of the {{nihongo|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji}} shrines and is considered one of the Five Main Ōji Shrines (五体王子, ''Gotai Ōji''). It is also known by alternative names such as '''Inamochi Ōji''' (稲持王子), '''Inaba''' (稲羽), and '''Inabane''' (伊奈波禰).<ref name="rekisi">Heibonsha (1997), p. 98.</ref> The site has been designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|和歌山県指定文化財一覧|1=list of Wakayama Prefecture designated cultural properties|2=ja|3=和歌山県指定文化財一覧|lt=Prefectural Historic Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11417412}} since April 1, 1958.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Cultural Properties: Historic Sites|url=http://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/mokuroku/kenkinen.html |publisher=Wakayama Prefecture Board of Education |accessdate=2009-02-07}}</ref>
== History == The shrine dates back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, with its first historical mention found in the ''{{nihongo|Chūyūki|中右記}}'' from 1109 (Tennin 2), referring to it as ''Inabane Ōji-sha''.<ref name="Kadokawa">''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten'' Editorial Committee (1985), p. 141.</ref> It is recognized as one of the quasi-Five Main Ōji Shrines in Emperor Go-Toba’s pilgrimage record from 1201 (Kennin 1),<ref name="rekisi"/> and by 1326 (Shōchū 3), it was formally acknowledged as one of the Five Main Ōji Shrines in the ''Kumano Engi'' held at {{nihongo|Ninna-ji|仁和寺}}.<ref name="Kadokawa"/> The shrine is also mentioned in the ''Kumano Pilgrimage Diary'' (1427, Ōei 34), which documented the pilgrimage of Kitano-dono, a consort of {{nihongo|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|足利義満}}.<ref name="Kadokawa"/>
The enshrined deity is a form of {{nihongo|Inari Okami|稲荷神}}, represented as ''Kongō Dōji'' (金剛童子) in the ''Kumano Engi''.<ref name="rekisi"/> A legend recounts that this was the site where the deity Inari Rōō (稲荷老翁) first met the Buddhist monk {{nihongo|Enchin|円珍}}.<ref name="rekisi"/>
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Inabane Ōji served as the {{nihongo|ujigami|産土神}} (guardian deity) of Iwata Village, with the main hall measuring approximately one ''ken'' square, as recorded in the ''Tanabe Domain Shrine Register'' (1792, Kansei 4).<ref name="rekisi"/> In 1915 (Taishō 4), the shrine was merged with {{nihongo|Iwata Shrine|岩田神社}}, and its structures were relocated the following year. However, in 1956 (Shōwa 31), a spirit of the deity was transferred back to the original site.<ref name="rekisi"/>
== Access == * Located in {{ill|Kamitonda, Wakayama|en|Kamitonda, Wakayama|qid=Q1346882|lt=Kamitonda, Wakayama|1=Kamitonda, Wakayama}}, near the Kumano Kōdō Nakahechi route. * Accessible via local transportation from {{ill|Tanabe Station|en|Tanabe Station|qid=Q135203|lt=Tanabe Station|1=Tanabe Station}}.
== Further Reading == * Heibonsha Editorial (1997), ''Daigo & Kii Temples and Shrines Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, ISBN 4582134025. * ''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten'' Editorial Committee (1985), ''Wakayama Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, ISBN 404001300X.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kumano shrine|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|qid=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} – {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30074932}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji}} : ({{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (田辺市・上富田町)|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|4=en|5=Kujūkuōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市・上富田町)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3200280}}) – '''Inabane Ōji''' – ({{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (田辺市・上富田町)|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|4=en|5=Kujūkuōji|error=九十九王子 (田辺市・上富田町)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3200280}})

This was a11ed to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines-->
